definitions.text: In a circle around; all round; on every side of; on the outside of; around. .mw-parser-output .defdate{font-size:smaller}
examples: The snake was coiled about his ankle.

definitions.text: Over or upon different parts of; through or over in various directions; here and there in; to and fro in; throughout.
examples: Rubbish was strewn about the place.
The children were running about the room.

definitions.text: Indicates that something will happen very soon; indicates a plan or intention to do something. See about to. On the point or verge of.
On all sides, or in every or any direction from a point; around.
examples: I looked about at the scenery that surrounded me.

definitions.text: Here and there; around; in one place and another; up and down.
examples: Bits of old machinery were lying about.

definitions.text: From one place or position to another in succession; indicating repeated movement or activity.
examples: walking about; rushing about; jumping about; thrashing about

definitions.text: Moving around or in motion; astir.
examples: out and about; up and about
After my bout with Guillan-Barre Syndrome, it took me 6 months to be up and about again.

definitions.text: In existence; being in evidence; apparent.
examples: This idea has been about for a while but has only recently become fashionable.

definitions.text: Near; in the vicinity or neighbourhood.
examples: I had my keys just a minute ago, so they must be about somewhere.
Watch out, there's a thief about.

definitions.text: To change the course of (a ship) to the other tack; to bring (a ship) about.
To about-face (turn 180 degrees, like a soldier).
